name: White-Chocolate Bread Pudding with Irish Whiskey
recipeIngredient: 2 cups milk (do not use low-fat or nonfat)
8 ounces day-old French bread, cut into 1-inch cubes (about 11 cups)
recipeInstructions:
For Sauce: Combine cream, whiskey and sugar in a heavy small saucepan. Scrape in the seeds from the vanilla bean; add the bean. Bring to a simmer over high heat, stirring constantly. Reduce heat to medium-low. Mix 2 tablespoons water and cornstarch in a bowl until smooth. Add to the cream mixture; stir until smooth. Continue adding to the cream mixture; let it simmer until the sauce thickens slightly and leaves a path when a finger is drawn across the back of a spoon, stirring frequently, about 10 minutes. Strain the sauce into a small bowl. Cover and refrigerate until well chilled, about 1 hour.
For Bread Pudding: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F (200 degrees C). Whisk milk, sugar, eggs, egg yolks, and vanilla in a large bowl until well blended. Mix in the bread cubes. Let stand for 20 minutes. Stir in both chocolates into the bread mixture. Transfer to an 8-inch square glass baking dish. Place the baking dish in a larger pan filled with enough hot water to come about 1 inch up the sides of the baking dish. Bake until the pudding is set, about 1 hour. Remove the baking dish from the water and let it cool slightly before serving warm with chilled sauce.
